reviews.review_text:
Our rooms were clean and felt fresh. Staff were friendly and accommodating. We stayed in a cabin with 1 queen bed and 2 singles. The bedrooms had no artwork on the walls and felt depressing due to the lack of colour. My only other complaint is the GuestCast.

Why have free wifi and a smartTV and lobotomise it? The smartTV has youtube, Netflix, Disney+ yet you cant use any of them because the TV cant log into the wifi. The GuestCast barely works and that took 20 minutes of fiddling to get it to do something. I am very tech literate and even I struggled. I can't imagine the stress it would cause a senior guest.

Fabulous park, very clean amenities and super excellent things for the kids to do and to play on. Top service and friendly staff. Located close to nowhere except the beach but it wasnt too far from Busso and it didnt matter anyway. Had an excellent weekend on a tidy caravan plot, my father stayed in a superb cabin. Recommend you give this one a try if you haven't already
Clean, spacious land, lots of thing to do with family. I liked our own camp-site bathroom. I would go there again and recommend it 👌
Stayed at an unpowered site, lovely environment and common showers + toilets were really clean with good hot water pressure.

We stayed in a one bedroom cabin which was perfect for our small family consisting of 2 adults, a toddler and a baby. There was plenty of room in the bedroom to set up the portacot and then there was a bunk bed in the hall way with a door separating it from the main living area.

We found this a great little cabin that was warm and had a good layout given it's small size.

Only thing I'd say is a deep clean is needed. While surface level, everything was clean, the wall by the toilet had brown marks on it around the toilet roll holder, the bathroom floor grout was thick with grime and all the window tracks were filled with dirt and the bathroom window sill had mold in the corners. Attention to these details would have made it a 5 star stay.
We are in our caravan nice clean park with nice facilities

Had ensuite caravan site it was
very well cared for,  shower toilet was very clean. It is well worth the stay!

Nice place to get away with the family, but some staff are rude and way too many rules, great for kids to interact with other kids, kids on bikes is what you see every second of the day, good facilities, laundry room available, toilets, showers and overflow car park, the houses on camp are nice and comfortable, has everything you need, town centre is close by, worth checking out, just be mindful that there are rules at camp and staff are very strict, if your kid is naughty, you'll be receiving a knock on your door or tent like we did lol
Exceptionally family friendly. First time for our children camping & they couldn’t have been happier! Great location & plenty of shade.

3rd year we had stayed at this lovely caravan park. Although there was a bit of mis communication regarding our site this year and we couldn’t get our repeat site. The sites we have been allocated were perfect. Fantastic kids activities, location to the beach and a quick ride to cafe and shops. Although there are some nasty reviews regarding Michelle we found her very approachable and engaging with our dogs and children. Showing them the new baby minion that had been born at the stop sign. Yes! there are noise restrictions after 10pm and yes! they are enforced. I would not like to have to be in the position to deal with the drunk, intimidating and rowdy people who do not respect these policies,  Can’t wait for next years trip.. if you stay here be kind and respectful and you will have a wonderful time!
Amenities are perfect! Great staff. Highly recommend

This park is on the outskirts of the main CBD of Busselton which made it nice to have that extra bit of a quiet stay.||We stayed for two nights on one of the camp sites.  Each site has a shad cloth type covering on the ground, so your tent or swag isn’t in the dirt.  There are peppermint trees providing some nice shade and well kept lawn.||The park grounds are very well maintained and staff are very friendly and welcoming.  ||There are two swimming pools, one being heated and the pool area is clean.||They have free Wifi and while they advise there could be some interruptions, we found we had good strong signal during our stay.||Now the camp kitchen.  This will not disappoint.  Nice open dinning area with plenty of table and chairs and if you like there is plenty of seating outside under cover.||The preparation - cooking area of the kitchen has two work areas each matched with a great oven, microwave, air-fryer, kettle, toaster, coffee pod machine (you of course supply the pods).  ||There are sufficient pots, pans and plates.||The park is dotted with a number of barbecue areas, with three just near the camp kitchen area.  These again like everything at the park, clean and very serviceable condition.  They are free for you to use.||While the bathroom blocks are dated, they are certainly clean.  The hot water is hot and pressure is good.||A short drive, or a 20 minute walk on a dual use path puts you at the Vasse Village Shopping Centre.  There are a number of shopping outlets.  A Coles grocery store, LiquorLand store and the Vasse store of the Bunbury Growers Market.  The Bunbury Growers Market will not disappoint and is worth a drop in, even for morning tea or lunch.||Beach is a 5 minute walk with calm waters and a nice white sandy beach.||This is an awesome park and a credit to the staff who never seem to stop all day.||Some may not agree, we liked it, but there are no e-bikes or e-scooters permitted to be ridden in the park.||There is a large bouncing pillow, play area, mini golf and other activities for the little ones.  They are positioned nicely in the park so they don’t impact on other guests.||Since the Busselton Bypass road has been in it has reduced the volume of traffic past the park which in turn makes it a little easier when turning right out of the park, especially with a caravan in tow.||Overall this is a great park.  We will certainly be back.

My wife and I just did a two night stay here on a camp site. I had a commitment which meant we had to return home or we would have extended our stay for the week.

Having changed from the caravan this was an awesome spot for our first run with our new tent.

We have travelled a lot and I always say you can generally tell how a park is from the entrance and the reception area.

Nicely set back off the road on a  sealed drive. The reception is clean and well presented with such welcoming staff.

Our camp site was very clean and all these areas have a good quality shade cloth style material on the ground, so your tent isn’t sitting in dirt.  Around you are beautiful trees providing some shade and fresh green lawns.  Each site is sufficient enough in size that you’re not sitting on top of other campers.

The camp kitchen was awesome and kept so clean, along with the grounds.  There is sufficient cooking facilities within the kitchen along with dishes, pots and pans.  The kitchen has two preparation areas mirrored with the same equipment.  Each having an awesome oven, cook top, microwave, kettle, air fryer and coffee pod machine (you of course supply the pods)

Large dinning area and plenty of seating with tables under cover outside.  The park is dotted with a number of barbecues and again spotless. There is no costs for the use of the barbecues.

Two swimming pools, heated and the area was very well maintained.

Great play area for the kids with mini golf and other activities.

Although the bathroom blocks are a bit dated, they are still very clean and nice hot water to the showers.

We found the Wifi excellent and sufficient signal.

We thought it was a good idea.  Within the park the use of e-scooters and e- bikes are not permitted and there is a strict 8 km/h speed limit.

A quick drive or a 20 minute walk on a sealed dual use path takes you to Vasse Village Shopping Centre where there are a number of retail outlets, Coles, Liquor Land and the Vasse outlet for Bunbury Growers Mart.

Beach is a 5 minute walk and the water was calm and very clear. Nice clean white sandy beach.

While this isn’t in the heart of Busselton, it is on the outskirts and the location is very rewarding to those staying here. It provides everything you need for a relaxing and wonderful stay.  It is nice to be just that little bit out of the heavy traffic.

Since they have put in the bypass road for Busselton, the road travelling past the park has less traffic, so turning right out of the park is a no stress.  Especially if you have a caravan behind you.

Overall this is a credit to the team who present this park.

Excellent job to the team.

Thank you and see you again for sure.
Manager Michelle aggressively enforced a 10pm curfew, yelling at quiet adult campers and waiting at our site before 10pm. Several campers were told to pack up unnecessarily. Unfriendly experience.
Great caravan park. Location is a bit out of town, but walking distance to a really nice beach. Plenty for kids to do as well!

Our recent stay at RAC Busselton Holiday Park was unfortunately very disappointing due to the behaviour of the park manager, Michelle.

At around 9:50pm, Michelle stood near our cabins while a small group of adults were quietly sitting outside talking. There was no music, no drunken behaviour, and no excessive noise. Despite this, we were told to go to bed at 10pm. Michelle remained standing at our seating area waiting for us to leave.

After we packed up and went to our campers we spoke to other campers who told us they had been given the same instruction — to stop gathering and go to bed at 10pm. Some people still wanted to sit and chat, but they felt forced to turn off their outside lights and sit in darkness whispering so they wouldn’t be confronted again as Michelle continued to patrol the area.

When walking back through the park later, other campers approached us asking if we had also been told to “go to bed and stop gathering.” Many people seemed shocked by the harsh wording and the way it was handled. At no point were the requests delivered with manners, respect, or kindness. Other holidayers described the experience as “being back at school and treated like children”.

To make matters worse, my 11-year-old daughter was followed back to our tent and yelled at while she was taking her scooter back from a friend’s campsite where it had been left earlier in the afternoon. (She was aware she couldn’t ride it at night, but was unable to carry it all the way back and ride it very slowly). Having a grown adult follow and yell at a child in the dark was terrifying for her and completely unnecessary.

The unisex bathrooms were also dirty, and there was very poor lighting at night, which made it uncomfortable walking there — particularly when the facilities are shared with the opposite sex.

After reading other recent reviews, it appears there has been a pattern of complaints over the last few months regarding the same manager and similar experiences where guests feel uncomfortable and unable to relax during their holiday.

Sadly, because of this experience, we will not be returning.

One of the most comfy beds of all times! I have stayed in 5 and 6 star hotels and resorts and the bed here tops them😀. Rooms very clean and tidy and well appointed. Staff very friendly and really appear to not just appreciate that they have a job, but really enjoy it - AND that shines through how the great the guests. Nice to see. Now that I have experienced this place it will be on my list of "will recommend and return" locations.
Camped here for one night and loved the place! Beautifully maintained, facilities are clean and the park has mini golf! Thanks 😊
Keurige camping met mooi zwembad.

We stayed at RAC busselton for 3 nights, and were immediately impressed by the customer service on arrival. We were greeted by Cheryl and Sue, who went above and beyond to make our experience comfortable and were very accomodating. The campground was well equipped, had everything we needed and the bathrooms were spacious and clean. We will be back!! Thankyou :)
Well presented park grounds, friendly staff. Thank you to the lovely lady in reception who went out of her way to enable us to extend our stay. We highly recommend this park.
Nice friendly place, good caravan bays, close to amenities 👍
Great park and friendly staff.
Great family park.

Pretty Disappointing. Unfortunately, the toilets and showers were not very clean during our stay and could definitely use more regular attention.
We also noticed some confusion around the pool rules. Guests were being told off for jumping into the pool, even though the sign only stated “No diving.” Clearer signage would help avoid misunderstandings and make the area feel more welcoming.
Overall, the park has potential, but improvements to cleanliness, facilities maintenance, and clearer communication from staff would make a big difference.

This was our second year camping over New Years at RAC Busselton. The first year was fabulous. It was such a disappointment this year compared to last year. The toilets and showers were absolutely revolting and were not being cleaned regularly. After blood was on the floor in one of the bathrooms for 30 hours, I approached office staff. No apologies, just annoyance at me pointing out they had not been cleaned. There was blood, toilet paper, mud, sand and excrement all over the place at different times. They told me they were understaffed and too busy cleaning the cabins. The staff they did have including Michelle and Cheryl seemed more intent on telling off children and threatening to kick them out of the campground if they were not wearing their yellow wristbands. They are not personable at all and treat the children and teens like criminals for merely acting like kids. The evenings were very loud and no one was policing this whereas the year before, they would walk around and ask people to be quiet after 10pm.  We left the campground 2 nights early due to how feral the facilities were and the lack of cleaning. I would not recommend as clearly it has gone downhill and is far too expensive for what you get. Such a shame.

Have been staying at this park since Peppermint Park days since RAC has taken over the park, the park has gone backwards, sites a dust bowl, dirty ammenties list goes on. It's a shame how they have let this park go into disrepair. New management/enforcer, remind me of the the witches from hocus-pocus it is funny watching kids scramble when they walk around pointing fingers, at least the last management could have a conversation with their guests. If you're looking for your kids to have fun maybe look elsewhere.

We stayed on a powered site for 7 nights, and the facilities were great! Clean and maintained to a very high standard. The bathrooms were cleaned thoroughly and regularly. We had the best time! The kids enjoyed themselves so much that we wished we could have stayed a few more nights!
Staff not too polite, also they were in my room one hour prior to check out.
Third year in a row & just keeps getting better, we love looking forward to ending the year here!
This place was great nice and clean and atmosphere seeing dogs allowed is great. Only thing is they should have a smaller pool for kids babies and toddlers as the pools very deep.

Lovely holiday park, and helpful friendly staff! We stayed in a cabin which was super clean and the services around the camp site were also very well kept and maintained throughout the day. Location of site is good for exploring Busselton and further into Margaret River.
I like this holiday park. Beautiful atmosphere and kid friendly pet friendly rooms are all clean and well kept
Great grassed camping area
Lawns needs some TLC. Water.pressure very low in ablution block. But we still enjoyed the park.
Lovely stay here for a week
Love this place

While we were using the communal area, a senior staff member named Michelle  approached us and advised that we were not permitted to remain there; however, the communication was handled in an unprofessional manner.
Definitely won't recommend this place if you are in different race.
Great place, heated swimming pools. Ideal for families with kids. Been there for a few times now. We'll maintained and safe

Absolutely awesome stay here at RAC Bussleton Holiday Park the past 3 nights! We stayed in a Queen Bed Studio that was exceptionally clean and well supplied. The cleaning staff should be commended on their attention to cleaning detail! Our room was impeccable! The grounds are well maintained with beautiful shady trees throughout! The 2 swimming pools are also meticulously maintained making our holiday experience so enjoyable! Michelle, Sheryl, Sara and Rebecca and the entire staff are friendly, personable and very professional! This park is a wonderful example of how holiday parks should be presented! Superb job! So much so that we have booked another visit in the coming months!

Spent two nights on an unpowered site, even though we were self sufficient the camp ground has everything you might need especially in the kitchen!
Stuff was very friendly and open to accomodate us with our requests.

Great for families, great for short or long stay, was very quiet and comfortable.
Plenty of things to do in the surroundings, at reception they also gave us a map and showed us some options for places to visit!

Will definitely visit again!

We had a camper van on a powered site that had a mat we could use to set up on.  The sites were spacious and had much appreciated shade trees we could utilise.
The bathroom facilities were old but clean.  The roomy, clean kitchen was well equipped but was a long distance away from our site.
It was a quiet location to enjoy a break from the road.

Absolutely beautiful park. Very clean amenities and very friendly staff. Lots of families visiting as well as other people just travelling through. Lots of things for the kids to do here. Jumping pillow, playground with lots of swings, adventure exploring, mini golf, volleyball, basketball, a games room, and a movie hub. As well as 2 swimming pools (one semi-heated), many amenities in various locations around the park - to accommodation full grounds. Feeding the Ducks by the lake was a big hit for my little ones, and the rabbits that came through of a night time. Very well set up grounds and very well kept. Will definitely return here again.

We are currently staying at this park and it is fantastic! There are honestly so many things for the kids to do from swimming to the bmx track to super nintendos in the games room. The staff here have been wonderful too. I was worried when I saw a bunch of bad reviews about a staff member named Michelle but I honestly think it’s more an issue that guests didn’t like having rules enforced. We made a mistake regarding visitors, Michelle explained the policy, I listened to her and made sure we stuck to the rules. Easy as! If you want to have a happy holiday, it also depends on your attitude.
The grounds are neat and tidy, ablutions are clean, bunnies are cute! What’s not to love?
Great Caravan park - dog friendly, big sites and a great laid out park.  Super close to the dog beach also.

Unfortunately I can only give this place a negative review, due to how rude the staff at reception are. Without a doubt the rudest staff I've encountered anywhere in the world in years.

We were a group of 12 people in our 30s - lawyers, teaches, doctors, journalists, business owners and other professionals. We are all respectful adults, on a road trip celebrating 2 people in the group's marriage. Many of us have camped all over Australia, so we know how to camp respectfully. However, we were all treated as if we were a bunch of unruly teenage hooligans from the start.

Upon check in, we enquired about the "1 car per site" policy. We enquired as to why it was the case and whether it was a space issue (we had 2 small cars that could easily fit onto a pitch). However, when we were told it was non flexible despite the size of car, we said that's fine, and my friend enquired about booking an extra site so she could sleep in her car. She was immediately dismissed, told "there's no space after tomorrow" even though we said we were only staying the one night. We said that, and we were dismissed again. The receptionist didn't even check the computer to see if there was space!

At this point, a lady named Cheryl started aggressively "telling us off" for enquiring, treating us as if we were teenagers. She told us that we would be kicked out if we parked more than one car on a pitch, in a very condescending tone. We said again, we were trying to PAY for an EXTRA pitch and then were dismissed. It turned out there were empty pitches right next to us that remained empty all night. Pitches were also bookable on the website, so we know that it wasn't a no-show.

Cheryl also said to another friend that quiet time was after 10, and "she didn't want to have to come and tell us to be quiet". Again, talking to us like we were children. We were actually all in bed by 9 as we were exhausted and are fully aware of camping etiquette!

She had a rude, condescending tone throughout every interaction. Whether it's because we looked "young" or she's just like that with everyone I do not know, but unfortunately it completely put me off the campsite and I can only say: stay away unless you don't mind being treated like a child at brownie camp.

I have stayed in other RAC holiday parks throughout WA and have loved them (and been treated like an adult at them all!) so don't let this tarnish your reputation of all RAC parks, but I would steer clear of RAC Busselton. To the park managers, I'd highly recommend basic customer service training and teaching reception staff how to add last minute bookings, as for some reason the other receptionist was sure she couldn't add one, despite half the park being empty.

Currently staying here, camping in a non-powered site…
The park is clean, very well laid out and heaps for the kids to do…
Staff are fantastic and I we will be back again 😊😊😊

Mooie locatie. Vriendelijk personeel.
Fijne camping.
Caravan parks is amazing.

Good caravan park for kids with cows and ducks to see. The playground is a bit outdated and has sand. The two-bedroom cabin wasn’t very clean — for the price, it really should have been. Mould in the bathroom, dust all through the runners. Hair on the toilet floor an dust.
Nice camping facilities. Kids are really happy around here

We stayed at RAC Holiday Park Busselton and honestly, it was a pretty disappointing experience.

Pros:
The park itself has some good stuff for kids — table tennis, basketball, a swimming pool, a games room, and a big bouncing pillow. The kids had fun and there’s plenty to keep them busy.

Cons:
The bathrooms and toilets really need an upgrade and better cleaning. Some taps were broken, hand wash wasn’t refilled properly, and overall maintenance felt neglected.

The worst part though was the customer service. One staff member, Michelle, made our stay really uncomfortable. She told us we couldn’t put our tent on the grass because it would “destroy the grass” — but then we saw plenty of other tents and even cars on the grass! Doesn’t make sense at all.

Later that night around 8 PM, she came into the games room, took the TV remote from the kids, and told everyone to leave because “the room closes at 5 PM.” When we asked where that rule was written, she said it’s on the website — which it definitely isn’t. She even admitted she let others stay late to watch the footy final, but for some reason kicked us out. Super rude and inconsistent.

Overall, great facilities for kids, but terrible customer service and poor maintenance. Michelle’s attitude completely ruined the stay for us. Would not recommend and definitely won’t be coming back.

——- Request for Clarification Regarding Rules and Fairness ——
Thank you for your response, but I would appreciate clarification on a few points. You mentioned that our group entered the games room outside its fixed hours — could you please share a direct link to where these hours or related rules are published on your website? You also stated that rules are applied equally to all guests, yet during our stay, we observed other visitors using the games room well past 5 PM without being asked to leave. Could you explain why exceptions appear to be made for some guests if fairness is being upheld? Additionally, while you cited rules about keeping vehicles on mats or designated areas, we noticed that nearly all other guests had their cars, campervans, or trailers parked directly on the grass without issue. I would appreciate a clear explanation as to why this rule was enforced selectively, seemingly only for certain visitors.

Michelle (Manager) runied my family holidays, if you are coming form the diverse backgrounds please avoid this place, they will make sure that they will give you a worst customer service.

In response to the owner comment!!!

Thank you for your response, but your message clearly avoids the actual issue I raised. My concern was not about the “style of stay” it was about the unprofessional and discriminatory behaviour shown by your manager, Michelle, which you have completely ignored.

Stating that your park “welcomes guests from all backgrounds” means nothing when the lived experience says otherwise. Instead of taking accountability, you have chosen to generalise and deflect responsibility.

I have stayed at many places across WA, and this is the first time I have felt unwelcome, disrespected, and singled out for no reason. If this is how RAC Busselton chooses to handle serious feedback, then it only proves my point that the issue lies not with the guests, but with the management culture.

I will be sharing my experience publicly so that other families, especially from diverse backgrounds, can make informed decisions before booking.

We recently stayed at the RAC Park and honestly, our experience was not pleasant. There were no proper baby rooms, which made things difficult as we have a one yr old. The ablution rooms were average. There were no hair dryers, and the overall cleaning was just average. However, the thing that bothered us the most was the behavior of one of the staff members, a lady named Michelle, who claimed to be the manager. She was extremely rude and seemed to target us several times during our stay.||At one point, she asked us to move our car from our site, even though it wasn’t blocking anyone’s way. Everyone else had their tents set up on the grass, but she specifically told us to remove and redo our tent setup because she claimed we were killing their grass, whereas other people's tents, caravans, and 4x4s were on the grass too, which made no sense at all. It really felt like she was trying to make things difficult for us. Later that same day, she came into the gaming room and snatched the remote from my 7year old son’s hand, speaking to us in a very disrespectful tone. When she asked where we were staying, I couldn’t recall our camp number immediately, and instead of being polite, she rudely said, “Which number, ma’am?” She then announced she was locking the room because it was “supposed to be locked at 5 pm,” even though it was already 8 pm. My husband witnessed the whole situation and got very upset, especially because the way she snatched the remote frightened our child. He confronted her and asked why she was behaving like this and targeting us since our arrival.||This entire experience really ruined our trip. Our kids were upset, and we couldn’t enjoy our stay at all. My husband was quite upset, as our son got frightened by the sudden and harsh interaction. It definitely affected our family’s overall experience and enjoyment of the stay. While the location and facilities are ok, I would recommend the management to ensure that staff interact with guests in a polite and understanding manner, especially with families and children. We truly hope our feedback helps improve the experience for others in the future.||If you’re planning to visit with family and kids, especially from diverse backgrounds, you should be mindful of this behaviour. The way we were treated felt discriminatory and deeply unpleasant.

We recently stayed at the RAC Park and honestly, our experience was not pleasant. There were no proper baby rooms, which made things difficult as we have a one yr old. The ablution rooms were average. There were no hair dryers, and the overall cleaning was just average. However, the thing that bothered us the most was the behavior of one of the staff members, a lady named Michelle, who claimed to be the manager. She was extremely rude and seemed to target us several times during our stay.
At one point, she asked us to move our car from our site, even though it wasn’t blocking anyone’s way. Everyone else had their tents set up on the grass, but she specifically told us to remove and redo our tent setup because she claimed we were killing their grass, whereas other people's tents, caravans, and 4x4s were on the grass too, which made no sense at all. It really felt like she was trying to make things difficult for us. Later that same day, she came into the gaming room and snatched the remote from my 7year old son’s hand, speaking to us in a very disrespectful tone. When she asked where we were staying, I couldn’t recall our camp number immediately, and instead of being polite, she rudely said, “Which number, ma’am?” She then announced she was locking the room because it was “supposed to be locked at 5 pm,” even though it was already 8 pm. My husband witnessed the whole situation and got very upset, especially because the way she snatched the remote frightened our child. He confronted her and asked why she was behaving like this and targeting us since our arrival.
This entire experience really ruined our trip. Our kids were upset, and we couldn’t enjoy our stay at all. My husband was quite upset, as our son got frightened by the sudden and harsh interaction. It definitely affected our family’s overall experience and enjoyment of the stay. While the location and facilities are ok, I would recommend the management to ensure that staff interact with guests in a polite and understanding manner, especially with families and children. We truly hope our feedback helps improve the experience for others in the future.
If you’re planning to visit with family and kids, especially from diverse backgrounds, you should be mindful of this behaviour. The way we were treated felt discriminatory and deeply unpleasant.

Hello, I have been on many holiday camps before and this is by far the worst!! There is a certain few staff who are absolutely horrible to visitors but especially children.
My kids were out at 8pm playing a game of chasy on the oval when a manager came up to them threatening to kick them out and told them to get back to there camps at 8pm when quiet time is 10. Then she went up to 2 small very quiet biker kids and made them go back to camps (they were around 7-9 years old)
My teen kids were on the jumping playing a game of memory tag where once your tagged you sit down until your tagger is down (so not long) and they got in trouble for sitting down on the pillow, my 13 year old son got told to get off the jumping pillow and read the rules out loud Infront of her and if he didn't they said they would not allow him to get on it again. Mind you I understand that teenage kids accidentally push little kids, bump them or run into them on accident but it was only them since they sent all the kids under 12 back to camp and continued to watch them at the gate for 10 minutes.

In conclusion not coming back because of that staff. Absolutely horrible on many occasions. Don't recommend if you have young kids and don't want to get yelled out.

We loved this caravan park. Heaps of kids, tents, dogs. It was chaos at times as it should be.

We had an ensuite which costs extra but due to a disability it made life easier for me. They were a little dated but they also supplied towels so not travelling with wet towels.

Heaps within the park for kids and adults to do. Mini golf, swimming pool, a bike track, a bounce pillow and more. They also have these peddle carts for 1 or 2 ppl that you hire and for use only in the park. Very popular so you need to book. They also had a movie night.

Son has booked again for Christmas holidays 🙂

Everything is good only office old big lady rude and resist
She supervised like her servant
Good location and services.
This is a lovely caravan park. The facilities are excellent and the people running it are a delight. They are very accommodating and helpful and all done with a smile.
Only problem we had was with the TV reception was buffering all the time so the internet needs to be looked at and problem resolved.

We had a great time in this park. Even extended our stay. Member’s Winter price was $28 per night for power & water. The caravan sites are large and easy to access with a big rig. Great super clean amenities and wonderful staff. The location is excellent too for touring the local attractions.
Später Check in und out war möglich in der nebensaison. Sehr nettes Personal!!!

We had a 2 bed cabin and it was excellent! Modern, clean and very well appointed, no different to staying at a fancy hotel. We arrived early and our room was already warmed on a cold and rainy day. The comms and tourist advice was excellent. If I could give 10 stars, I would. Highly recommended.

The one bedroom chalet we stayed in was perfect for a long weekend away. It had everything you needed for a cosy stay and was spotlessly clean and tidy. The park staff were very welcoming and friendly and the location was quiet and beautiful. Not within walking distance of Busselton, but an easy 10 minute drive, with a local IGA, coffee shop and bottle shop, 3 mins drive down the road.
Very good
It is the best stay that I had for the 5 days. I would recommend this place to stay at.
Top class caravan park with great member and off peak rates
Great place and great staff . Definitely going again
Great facilities kids loved the pedal carts. The cabins were nice and comfortable.

So it's disappointing to have to write this review. We checked in and arrived at our site and plugged in, only to have the plug blow out straight away. We noticed the plug was black. We reported this to the office and got blamed for somehow blowing it up, which is completely incorrect and quite simply unsafe that these basic items are not checked after people check out. After some painful discussions with the front office, we were able to sort out the sites we had to keep us all together. Furthermore, a few of the kids were playing spotlight (the aim of this game is to hide and seek quietly), so not making a lot of noise, but management still came over at 8 PM and said, "They can't be running around the park. I know it's not 10 PM, but we can't have them running around." So, there was no complaint about noise, just don't have fun after dark. The caravan park is quite empty, and they really are not making noise.
Beautiful caravan park. Loads of facilities. Only con, just a bit too far from Busselton centre and restaurants.
Great place to stay and great location.
Good park. Lots of room. Plenty of activities for kids and room to separate the old folks
It was a great experience. I stayed in a powered site. The restroom was clean, and the receptionists were all very welcoming!
It was great experience for us as the beginner campers. Powered and well equipped camp ground. We had so much fun in the park.

This park has it all. An amazing camp kitchen, games room, playground, 2 pools, one is heated, and great sites for all different campers. Our ensuite had fresh towels, lovely shampoo, conditioner and body wash. Plenty of covered ares with BBQs for eating outside. Great place to stay.
Perfect for a family holiday
Absolutely gorgeous if you have kids it has plenty to do. Staff are always helpful. The cabin is fully equipped and comfortable.
Pet friendly caravan park.

RAC Busselton Holiday Park is a gem! The location is perfect—peaceful yet close to attractions. The facilities are clean and well-maintained, with great amenities for families. Friendly staff go above and beyond to ensure a relaxing stay. A fantastic spot for a getaway in beautiful WA! Highly recommend.
Great facilities but the staff could do with reminding that they are running a holiday park and not a maximum security prison
Was a little out of town but was ok for us ,

We stayed in an ensuite site which was awesome! Kids loved the bike track and the jumping pillow.
Camp kitchen well equipped
Only downside was the entertainment room... none of the games appeared to be working and other games just looked past their best
Really nice relaxing spot and great for exploring busso
Very friendly and quiet. Lots of shade and grass. Clean amenities.

We had a powered caravan site. They are soft padded areas for your  comfort and the sandy area for the caravan. We were near an ablution block and laundry. Not the most modern facilities but still very good.
We stayed more days than booked as there is so much to see in the area. The camp kitchen is great. Has all you need if you have arrived with nothing other than the food you want to cook. There are 2 swimming pools but 20° is too cold for us. A good holiday camp.
Great caravan park for families. The sites are spacious and there are a variety of activities for the kids to do including minigolf, box, bouncing pillow, playground, pool and more

Very busy during school holidays. Caravan sites extremely sandy and annoying. Loud kids still going on at 10pm.
Location not close to Busselton.
Shower facilities always messy due to the sand, a challenge for the staff to keep clean.
It was just perfect place to stay, 5 stars for any reason and any family.

Well, RAC Busselton is so beautiful. The facilities are ok and in good condition. Toilets and showers keep clean and tidy. If you need to use the kitchen camp, you can use the microwave, air frier, kettle, and rice cooker. There are three fridges, bring your name badge 😉  to put into the fridge. There are a few bbq, small pool,

Lovely place
Can get loud with kids at night but part of the experience
Amazing loved it just need to share the love ❤️
Owner repeatedly shut down advertised services in the park. Specifically the bouncing pillow. Do not let these activities affect your decision to come here as she’ll probably close them anyway
We camping here, close to beach, very convenient and clean!  👍and nice staff 🎊

We had a wonderful stay at the RAC Busselton holiday park.
We met the staff on check in ( Sarah ,Vivianne and Jane ) who were very polite and helpful.
Our accommodation was a 2 bedroom cabin that was immaculately clean and comfortable.
The Kitchen was very functional and well stocked. Overall we felt very relaxed and at home.
We met the site manager Michelle and her husband Peter who made our stay an enjoyable one in every way.
The park was very neat, clean and easy to navigate. We Will definitely be visiting again and would highly recommend to everyone.
Great experience. Very nice place to stay. They have kitchen, gaming room and lake view.
Loved our stay, we will come again. Park has plenty of shady spots, it's calm and very clean. The 2 pools are really nice, amenities are clean at all times. Recommend 😉

A nice home away from home. Security screens on the windows and doors would eliminate any safety concerns. The rooms are quite stuffy at night with them locked up. It would be nice to keep windows open whilst sleeping.

Spent 5 nights in a cabin
It was spotless.the toiletries they supplied were beautiful.
The only downside was the pool, play ground and jumping pillow closed so early on hot nights. The kids were disappointed after spending all day out to not be able to enjoy these facilities while parents relaxed a bit.
It was advertised as dusk but were closed a lot earlier
Trees need underpruning. Grass virtually non existant. Staff need to study the rules so they dont misquote them.

Very disappointed!|We were so looking forward to our 7 night stay in the Caravan Park. Being an RAC Park & from the photos on the website, it looked so nice with grassy sites.|Our site had ripped matting, was dusty & had dry sand that blew dust up everywhere. It didn't have any grass. In fact, there are very few site with grass, most are mainly sand with ripped matting. The whole park looks very unkept.|You will need to bring your own, very big, floor mat with you to put on your site.|But the ablutions are clean & there is a nice camp kitchen, but no oven.|Good facilities for the kids with pool  & playground.
Excellent location and facilities. Highly recommend an ensuite bathroom. Very clean.

Hi we just got back from a great long weekend with you and the park and customer service is amazing! The only issue was the children and their bikes, unfortunately this year there was a large volume of kids riding bikes in the dark with no lights and no parent supervision which ended up in one of our family members being hit by a bike and hurt. I was under the impression bikes were not to be ridden in the dark however unfortunately some guest don’t seem to listen and ruin a fun night out for others
The children’s bikes were also parked in the middle of the roads which was a cluster of 10 plus bikes which lead to us not be able to drive to our site and again no parent supervision was around for us to ask for the bikes to be moved. I completely understand the fun of the bike rides with friends we just need to be mindful of all ages.
Can’t wait to come back next year 😊
Nice caravan and camping sites, very helpful and friendly staff.
Absolutely brilliant.
A few k's out of town but a good park. Has 2 pools & ocean is walking distance away

Lovely receptionist but the sandy sites are awful 😞. Sand blows into everything, especially if you put washing out to dry. Wifi doesn't work. Disappointed particularly as two previous parks in Dunsborough and Albany were so green and lush, which shows the staff have pride in these places. This caravan park is depressing.

Calm relaxed atmosphere with green sites and lots of shade from the trees.

Pool is cleaned daily with clean and well looked after amenities.

Lovely place to stay at
Staff were friendly and helpful
Close to heaps of places and also alot to do there

Recently took the kids down for a quick getaway before school went back, so much for the kids to do with the jumping pillow, pump track, mini golf, pool and more. Easy walk to the beach and the ladies at the front desk were lovely when we got home and realised my son had forgotten his new hat that he got for Christmas at the jumping pillow. I called the day we left (mon) to ask if it had been handed in and they called me back the next day (tues) to say they had found it and would post it back to Perth for us. It arrived Thursday so I had one very happy boy when he got home from school that day. Thank you ladies very much!

Definitely somewhere we would stay again!

When I booked the accomodation on the 20 December 2024 I was charged on my credit card $346.01 for a one night stay at the Rac Busselton Caravan Park , on arrival on the 27 Dec 2024 was charged again an amount of $342.55 , and informed there was insufficient funds at the Trip. Com booking .I am now caught up in a dispute between Trip.com and the caravan park . The caravan park is saying they have not received payment from Trip.com and I have been charged  twice for the accomodation . I am deeply disappointed in this and are waiting for a refund
Over priced. Toilet's and showers not to the standard you would expect from a RAC caravan park. The sites are average, not up to the standard of other cheaper caravan parks.

January 2024 was our fourth visit to RAC Busselton. Every year the decline in the park grounds is more evident. We always stayed in a cabin. The first time we came the cabin was absolutely filthy. Everything was terribly dirty and the dust was piled up in the wardrobes and there was even dust on the walls. The bathroom and toilet had been ‘cleaned’ but the years of caked on grime was still stuck everywhere in between grooves and on taps and corners. I have the photos to prove it, should RAC require it.

There was maltezers and months of dirt under the bed when I urgently had to pull it away to chase after a runaway cockroach. Then I had to go on a cleaning spree before we were feeling a bit more comfortable. That year there was still a bit of grass left around the unit. We rebooked as we enjoyed coming with friends who camp on powered sites close by. I asked the staff to clean the unit properly before we arrived, which they did.

Every year after that, there seemed to be a decline in the quality and quantity of the offerings in the unit.
The kitchen has the bare, bare minimum to say the least. One very large pan, one very small pot ??? (used to have 3 sizes when we first came) one very large strainer ??? No salad bowl, no spatula, no lid for pan, all of the cutlery and glasses are mixed and nothing matches. Only 5 of each so you have to constantly wash up.

The fridge was dirty, which I had to clean first. There was a hair in the freezer compartment. This makes me think that the cleaning staff did not even open the fridge to inspect it. The one rack of the door in the freezer compartment is also now missing and has not been replaced.

The storage for clothes and stuff is also very limited. Although the robes are large, there is no shelving in the kids bedroom at all. Everything has to be kept on the floor of the robe or in a suitcase or bag. The night stand has no drawer and is very small with just enough space for the lamp. This is quite inconvenient.

The main bedroom robe has a 3 drawer unit with very limited storage and the drawers only opens halfway. This is very limited storage for two people who stay for a week. The robe also only has 5 hangers between 2 people. There is also no other place for storage of a vanity case, make up, toiletries, creams hair brushes etc. As the storage in the bathroom is very limited as well, everything can’t be stored there.

There is literally no more grass left around our unit or in 90% of the park. It is all just now a black dusty sad area that makes everything black and dirty. There are no gardeners or groundskeepers working about like you would see in some other parks we visit. No sprinklers running and no maintenance or gardening done to make it a happier looking place. Just a sad sad look overall. Last year we laid tarp in-front of our unit to prevent the sand and dirt coming in you thongs but this year we’ve just given up. I’ve decided to write this review after I saw the beautiful aerial photo of when it was still Peppermint Park back in 2014 when all the grass was still there and nice and green. I came to read other reviews and saw many others complaining of the same issues and even people saying management not wanting staff to water the park. It is just very sad to me that such a once beautiful lush green place had to decline like this.

The pool area is also very meh compared to other parks’ pool areas.

These issues are such easy fixes. RAC charge the same as other much greater parks. What are they doing with the income they’re getting?

The absolute decline of this park in only 4 years is very shocking and sad. I won’t recommend it even to my enemies LOL 😂

There are a few positives though: the toilet and shower have been replaced so that is great and that old grime is gone, but that is it.

But Sadly we’ve decided not to return as there are more enjoyable parks that will cost us the same.

We stayed here for 8 nights during the Christmas 2024 period.

Biggest compliment to ground staff Dan. He was extremely wonderful with the kids and giving us tips on what to do. He also came to see us on our last day to see if he can lend us a hand. Absolutely wonderful bloke!

Love:
Daily kids activities
Pool
Clean grounds

Things that can be improved on:
Amenities - understandably it’s the busy season but the female toilets had two working cisterns, and it was rather filthy
Laundry - the top loaders were dirty

I want to start by saying I stayed here a few years ago for Christmas and it was excellent.  Unfortunately I noticed a definite decline in the park overall. We were situated in a wind tunnel with black sand completely surrounding us and so many holes in the green matting on our site that it did little to stop the sand blowing everywhere, all over my camp pantry and kitchen.  I do have a huge tarp and gazebo but it was blowing over from other black sandy sites or being walked in as there was no grass around us. The toilets were dirty almost all the time. One of the sinks in the disabled toilets stank to high heaven (which I was instructed to use when they were  'cleaning' the other toilets). None of the buckets ever had any soapy water in them so I couldn't mop before or after showering. The floors were always filthy, toilet paper every where and excrement down all toilet bowls. At night ducks would come onto our site doing their business around our chairs and tables (no I did not leave out food scraps ever!) Disgusting to wake up to as you exit your tent.
But the worst of it was how Very rude Michelle in the office was. All other staff were absolutely lovely but Michelle's comments were uncalled for, her tone rude and her demeanour unwelcoming. I will Never stay here again and I will Never recommend it to anyone.

Jane and Michelle were incredibly helpful! Both gave us a last minute stay when we were unorganised, and also allowed us to occupy a space nextdoor so we werent so crowded! Really kind and sweet souls, would recommend 10/10
Amazing time with the boys

My family and I recently stayed at this holiday park in a cabin costing us $3,340 for 6 nights, 7 days

Everything was going great until we realised the kitchen has minimal items to cook with and use such as a small pot won’t work for a family of six
The bathroom no matter if fan n window was open n on would fog up with so much steam it was unbearable

The Netflix is only for kids movies

The hub when it had movies the children had to sit in a row on hard plastic chairs like in a military camp

Michelle the manager was rude, unprofessional and had no joy or spark in her
Her top was always filthy with food
She would hide behind palm trees and trees spying on ppl in pool area which closed at 6pm even on 40 plus degree days
The inflatable pillow n playground shut down at 6pm sharp
The black sand outside our cabin was a nightmare and the cabin floors were filthy upon arrival as our white socks ended up black
She would drive around counting chairs in your area and spy on ur area of where ur staying
Kids were not allowed to ride scooters etc after 7pm
And if u we’re out on ur cabin balcony at 10pm having quiet drinks she would tell u to go inside as it was quiet time and time to shut down like we were teenagers
Michelle was difficult to talk too and numerous other people mentioned the same issues
Would we return again, hell no never again
Felt like we couldn’t enjoy ourselves completely and the tiling around the pool was dangerous and tiles were lifting up
Michelle even said she didn’t want kids jumping in the pool as the water level would get low so what! Kids can’t have fun n expected to doggy paddle around in circles in the pool

We tried to give this feedback in person on Leaving but reception just said ok I’ll let her know

Spending so much money like we did we expect better but the point is no whatever how much you spend $5 or $500 u expect better

Amazing for kids to meet new friends. Make sure they bring helmets, even for scooters, to avoid the fun police 😅 Gorgeous cabins all recently refurbished as of end 2024. The shower rose & recess are both HUGE!!! Water pressure excellent. Well appointed, simply. BYO scissors. The inflatable pillow is a rare treat, with the current national shift for insurers to steal them away from great holiday parks like this. Even some of the unpowered camping spots are top notch. That's the camping pic.

Since writing this we are back staying again for another four nights. Pricey being Christmas but we weren't expecting the gift arriving inside our cabin from RAC. It was extremely generous, and super cool 😎 A First Aid Kit for families! Huge! Why this fantastic is because an unexpected value adding to guests means you WANT to promote them. It's word of mouth that sells and a good reputation is hard to keep these days. But that is not why I wrote this 😆
Water quality shocking, Wi Fi Shocking, location & Staff Great

As we've never been to Oz or had a campervan holiday, i didn't review it until a week or so later, but at the time I said if all the sites are this good I'd be a happy bunny. They have been!  Only downside is there is very little to walk to for eating out.

Worker there named Michelle very bad worker very rude had a terrible experience ever since she joined my kid got in trouble about every night we stayed there that he can’t be outside without parents or with bikes around 7 also closing the trampoline for the kids about the whole day because there was two kids that where being silly on it could have just talked to them.
Amazing location. Perfect for down time
Great staff and good utilities

A quick getaway with my hubby and cousin to Busselton for three nights. It is great that they have charging ports for EVs, the room wasn't bad, in fact the room we got was quite good. It was clean, had the essential things like coffee, tea and sugar, air-conditioning, tv, hair dryer and cookies and 2 small milk in the fridge. The staff are very helpful and informative, they also had such nice location with a playground, man made lake, the cute little ducks and bunnies roaming around the park, we had a great stay!
Stayed 2 weeks great place. Everything you expect in a caravan park was 1st class. Site was large and staff excellent. Close to beach short drive to Coles and shops fully recommend

Bien ubicado de la playa pero lejos del centro (donde están los supermercados o el jetty) pero el campamento es re limpio re lindo. El baño que es lo clave impecable. La señora de la entrada súper amable.

Great area out the back to walk dogs. Great beach just 400 metres away.
Walking track out back to coles, McDonalds and coffee shops, about 20 minute walk.
Pool was good and good games room for kids.

Worst RAC Campground for me so far. Site was ok. Showers were dirty, dead flies everywhere. The laundry was well equipped for an appliances museum and expensive with 5$ for a load. Pool and Playground closed too early. Do not recommend.

Lovely park. Clean, quiet and friendly people at Reception. Didnt get to use a lot of their facilities since we were there just for overnight stay. But the toilets and laundry are clean and functioning well. Recommended!

We stayed at the Ensuite site using our rooftop tent for the night.
The staff were very friendly and helpful as it was our first time there.
The RAC holiday park is very nice and clean with a very handy kitchen & bbq.
There’s also a heated pool and other activities to do.
It pays to be an RAC member and get a good deal for the stay.

Rooms stink, hair all over the bathroom and beds, fridge stinks couldn’t put anything in it, drains stink.
Went into reception to buy a few ice creams for my family and the staff muttered to each other that the tills were balanced and “they always come in just before close” I was paid and out by 6 which is when you close.
Balance the tills after close would be my solution.
A bloke came in to check in, other staff member audibly sighed.
Paid $270 for the privilege, won’t be back.
The place is absolutely clean and well maintained.. neighboring caravan owners are friendly and quiet.. all amenities are available. loved the space we got!! ❤️
Good location, hires a campervan site. Reasonably close to the excellent Busselton jetty precinct
Clean accommodation, friendly service. Beautiful quite location
Lovely clean, well cared for site. Made even better by the possum coming to visit us next to our car in the evening.

We had a great time during our recent stay at RAC Busselton's ensuite camp. The ensuite was very clean and well-maintained, which made our stay comfortable and convenient. The campground was perfect for families with young children, with plenty of safe and grassy areas for our little one to play. The overall atmosphere was peaceful and relaxing. We plan on returning for future camping trips!
Excellent place , Love the walking trails and walk to beach .
Love this place
Couldn't fault anything with our stay. Had plenty of activities to keep boys entertained in school holidays

We stayed here for 2 nights and absolutely loved it.  The park itself is a little way out of time which makes it ideal to have a rest for all the hussle and bussle.  Check in is a breeze with the staff being amazing.|The sites are spacious and easy to get into with grass on one side and a grass pad on the other.  Lots of room to spread out|Amenities are so clean and fresh with lots of hot water and pressure.|The Laundry facilities are great and easy to use.|Lots to do and see in Busselton and surrounding areas.|Highly recommend staying here when in the area

Good facilities
Easy access to all facilities
Super good service from check in to parking , the only one with such a good service .
Super clean and convenient
Friendly to children
Friendly staff try to accommodate us
Sure will be back again .
5 star ⭐️
Over expected. All good, peace and quiet.
New

Absolutely brilliant park that is well maintained and lots of activities for children. Clean amenities with grassy sites and well spaced out. There's pathways beside the canal that goes to Vasse (Coles, McDonald's, bakery and a couple of other shops) approximately 1 km. If you go in the other direction, there's a path that leads to the beach but be careful as you have to cross a highway. The staff, especially Cheryl, are friendly, helpful and happy. Powered site is $41 with discount.

We just had a 5 night stay here with a group of 8 caravaners. The staff were very accommodating & put us in a group site together. The park is well maintained & ablutions cleaned daily. Facilities are great with plenty of BBQs, games room & camp kitchens.I would definitely recommend this caravan park to friends.
Womderful

Nice, comfortable chalets at a not too bad price per night. The service is fantastic too. Had a bit of a mix up with our booking but the staff were very accommodating and got us sorted in no time. Also threw in a free welcome hamper because of it, so absolutely no complaints. Otherwise the site is nice and quiet, and only a ten minute drive from the centre of town, so all things considered, very much recommended.

Fantastic staff, lovely park visitors, really awesome pooch and the odd kitty visitors. Oh and cant not mention the very friendly magpies and willy wagtails and other bird varieties, beautiful bush setting and very close to Coles Vasse.
Excellent walk and bike paths EVERYWHERE
highly recommended
Room basic but very comfortable very clean great value

We loved this park away from the main centre but not far to drive back in large sites clean bathrooms  great camp kitchen games room and movie room two small pools and bike track next door the staff friendly and helpfull

Zatrzymaliśmy się w tym miejscu na jedną noc w strefie unpowered. Bardzo sympatyczna obsługa 😀 Jeden z lepszych Holiday Park, w których nocowaliśmy 😀👍 Bardzo dobre wyposażenie! Przyjemnie spędziliśmy tutaj czas! Zdecydowanie polecam 😀
Lovely and relaxing clean and well maintained kids area fabulous
Lovely winter weekend away with the pooches
Great park. Staff are very friendly and welcoming. Have a great stay.

We stayed in a two-bedroom cabin and had a great weekend away. The cabins were great, with a nice hot shower and clean. The beds were comfy, and they had everything we needed. It is a short stroll to the beach across the road, where we went fishing. Staff were friendly, and the amenities are good - we will definitely be back.
The park is great for our 4, 12 and 14 year old kids.

Recently stayed her in our camper trailer with and it was awesome. Great customer service with friendly staff. Great for the kids with a bike track out the back, games room and a hub with arts and crafts on the school holidays. Very tidy clean park.

A nice 16 minute drive to Busselton town centre or 20 minutes away from dunsborough. We will definitely be returning here for many more family trips.

Cheers
Top spot. Excellent service. Convenient to all the Down South spots.
Great spot clean and tidy and staff that went over and beyond to help 👍

Amazing Customer service!
Spoke with Melissa over the phone and she was so friendly and accommodating. We are travelling with a boat and she made accommodations for us to park it somewhere we can see it from the cabin.
Extremely friendly staff!

Just stated they over the long weekend, found it rundown sandy, only 3x mens dunneys at our end of the park should have more for the size of the park, if your have a shower and if someone flushers the hot water goes from your shower,  pool and bouncy pillow close most of the time with no rebate for this, kids going 50kph on E scooters, had a bottle nocked off over night from out table, would stay some were else next time, give it a miss!

I only gave a one star for service as services also cover wifi availability.
Yes the lights were on but no one was home.
If you are going to advertise wifi as a feature that is available then please, ensure it is working to optimally performance. Yes some internet services are slow but this was slower than none existence.
A word to staff was basically shrugged off as   It is what it is.

Great location, great accommodation however the blinds need updating so the windows are covered and the fans are all doing different things to what the switch says, off is high, high is off, low is off and the forth setting is a slow broken try. The DVD player is gone so not much to watch at night once the kids go to bed, glad I brought my book. Also could have been a bit cleaner, 3 nights for almost $700 I expect a bit more. Oh and the fly screen doesn't lock from the outside.

We originally planned to stay 4 nights but had to extend to 6 due to car issues and while we were stressing about our car we could not have asked for a better place to stay. The ladies at reception were so kind to us throughout, even helping us transport things from our car (which was stuck in the carpark) to our caravan using their ute. Extending our stay was effortless and we were very pleased with the promo they had - we stayed 6 nights and only paid for 4. Fantastic value. We had an unpowered site overlooking the lake which was beautiful and populated with lovely bird life! The amenities were spotless and we were never bored by making the most of the mini golf and ping pong table. The location is slightly further from the CBD but this allowed for spacious sites and a relaxing atmosphere. We saw the van parks in town and they were overcrowded (and overpriced). If I return to Busselton I would stay here again and I recommend others do the same. You will not be disappointed! Thank you RAC!!

The staff were so incredibly friendly and helpful! After a bit of a glitch with Expedia the team were prompt to get it resolved for me! The cabin it self was lovely, very modern interior and everything you could need! Will absolutely stay again!

Great facilities but our caravan site was way overused and just sand with a ratty old shade cloth ground mat instead of concrete. I'd expect better grass maintenance at these rates. Also the van behind us had to run their grey water on the ground as the drain was the wrong side for them and too far away for a standard length hose.

Well managed site. We were there over a long weekend but not school holiday. They still have some activities for kids to join. Movie night, decoding, colouring. They also have donut truck, coffee truck, hot dog truck and pastry truck on site for some time.
This park is amazing for the kids. So much choice of stuff to do. Facilities are clean and fab. Will be back again!

I've been here a few times now. Great location out of the hussle bussle of the town centre but not too far away. We were relegated to the back half of the site due to the sheer size of our group (approx 40 people across 10 families)  it was I'm afraid a dust bowl compared to the front half of the park. Possibly due to the long summer we've had.  The ablution blocks were below par. 8 shower / toilet blocks for the very many spots, nowhere near enough facilities. Some days, no hot water or simply no water pressure and some in a bit of a grotty state. Also found no hot water in the camp kitchen on some days. For the shower blocks, a mop and bucket should be available for a quick mop up after use. That was probably the only real letdown, but other than that, the park has great facilities for the kids. Would have given it 5 stars if it wasn't for the poor condition of the few shower blocks available to the rear half of the park.

Amazing location and site
All amenities were nice
Toilets and showers were clean
Very kid friendly

Neglected
kitchen and toilet blocks very dirty

It's an absolute waste of your valuable time to visit this place. Instead of enjoying ourselves with friends, we're being constantly harassed. While we're here to have a good time, we're constantly interrupted and told to sleep every hour. What is this nonsense? Why bother visiting if we can't even enjoy some freedom and relaxation from our busy lives? This park is far from family-friendly, boss. Think twice before considering a visit.

We were being pressured to sleep by 10 pm, while everyone else was awake and seemingly focused on us for some reason. What's even more frustrating and downright ridiculous is that they claimed the swimming pool was heated, but they hadn't bothered to turn on the heating at all. It's baffling why we had to repeatedly ask for it. We tried twice, but no luck. They kept saying the supervisor wasn't on site. We won't be returning to this place. The site is unclean, poorly maintained, and covered in dust. We had a much better experience at Big4 last time and thoroughly enjoyed it there.

From my experience the workers at this park were so rude and mistreating, I just had one of the workers come up to our group and told us that our children were out past curfew, which was supposed to be 10pm, but the current time was 9:15?? The workers also stopped me and my kids from walking to the bathroom at night and we had to go back to our campsite.

Would not recommend to anyone to stay here, go somewhere else

I ain’t ever staying here again with THESE PEOPLE.
Friendly, sites great size, showers very clean.

Well maintained caravan park. Clean ensuite for caravan or campers.
Two pools, kids playground, fun activities area, excellent camper's kitchen and lounge.
Convenient location for regional holiday towns and sights  nearby in Busselton,  Dunsborough,  Yallingup and more.
Traffic noise isn't far away as is on a busy road.
Popular,  so book ahead!

Lovely CP big Caravan sites. Amenties are pretty good 👍 it was very busy but didnt feel hemmed in.
Recommend a stay here. Its our 3rd time here.
Stayed on a caravan power site. Plenty of room, clean amenities in a great location. Felt the park was a bit price but had everything needed with friendly staff.
Very nice experience. Nice to receive a welcome note and and when we left. Not .many places are so welcoming with great staff. Would thoroughly recommend.

1st time visitor to RAC and was super impressed with everything. Large sites for caravan and parking close to amenities. We didn't leave the site! 🤣 grandchildren had an absolute ball. Highly recommend. Will definitely put any RAC on our travel plans.

The great thing about RAC Busselton is you don't need to leave the park as majority of vav sites have there own sand pit. Great to walk on on a hot day. Put the sprinklers on to try to resuscitate the grass but worker turned it off.
Some areas that are general areas for use around camp kitchen and park reception are beautiful and green, well kept etc but van areas and dog van areas are disgusting.
People enjoy sitting under awnings enjoying breeze but not with the dust, leaves etc.
Toilets need a upgrade also but are clean and average caravan park quality.
This is our  3rd time here and it is getting progressively worse.
Need to rotate areas for vans and close sections off for it to regrow.
Money comes first but will loose in long run because people won't return. We'll seel better grounds next visit to Busselton.
Note: Office staff are brilliant and helpful shame others let the place down.
Well run park...good/clean amenities.  ..2 pools in good surroundings.  ..dog friendly in usual areas.

2 good size pools plenty of parking at the front. Lovely staff everywhere. The caravan park is kept very clean . Needs a little bit of upgrading in some parts . But overall it's lovely for a weekend or holiday. Close to the beach and a little drive to the town.

The park was great for kids and location.

The powered sites are also very good size, BUT the bathrooms are horrible

Bathrooms on one side being renovated, so there are not enough, they  put down gross portaloo that stinks in this heat.

Had to wait in line to use bathroom in the morning, 2 toilets not working made it worse, bathrooms need updating ASAP.

I would think it is common sense if they are renovating the one large bathroom to not book out the park to the max, that is just stupid!

This has been the most disappointing trip I've had in the last 4 years  we have been coming here. Right from the start, the main lady in the office was abrupt sour  and non helpful. There are limited  absolution block facilities at the moment,  sometimes waiting 15 to 20 minutes in the morning to get into a toilet cubical because of the line and only three cubicals. People drinking in the pool areas, from glass stubbles must considered a safety hazard. Personally I find it below the standard I have come to expect from an RAC park, we deserve  better, but my wife has stated to give them an go as usually this park has been a great holiday.
Great place the kids loved it ! Great for all ages. Two pools and plenty of play areas for the kids.  Including playground,  bouncing pillow and mini golf .

Enjoyed our stay on an ensuite site.
The park is beautiful and the staff were lovely.

Netter Park. Wir waren wahrscheinlich am falschen Tag da. ( Australiaday) und haben deswegen 95$ pro Nacht für ein unpowerd site bezahlt. Viel zu viel!
Aber der Pool ist toll. Außerdem mochten die Kinder den gamingroom.

This was once a great caravan park, nice grounds well maintained. Unfortunately RAC has managed to run it into the ground over the past 6 years. Grounds are beyond repair and general park cleanest is disappointing.

All you need for a family getaway, great camping areas, the shacks looks roomy and all the facilities were clean and tidy. Heaps of stuff for the kids to do and stay entertained, only minutes from local hot spot beaches, bars restaurants, and everything ells Busso has to offer.
Awesome customer service.  Nice 2 pools, tidy toilets. Definitely  will go again.

Don't get the powered site number 188.
Tiny corner spot, dead grass, very sandy and the unsealed sandy track right across sends dust and dirt into your van, face and food. The ablutions are not so nice this side of the park either. They are toilet/shower combined, and only eight of them. Which means only 8 people this side of the park can utilise them at once... annoying waiting for the loo whilst others are having long showers. Not sure why this spot costs the same as the nicer, better sites!

Lot of activities for kids (2 pools, indoor area, trampoline) . Walking distance to the beach. Lots of natural shade from trees. The camping lots are on a smaller side (for caravans). Facilities are not new but clean.

We really enjoyed our stay at the RAC Busselton. Yes the park gets to capacity over the weekend, but when we were there, it still had a great feel.
We liked the vibe.
Most if not all sites have mats down, but they are starting to look old a needing replacement after lots of people put pegs through

Our only complaint would be that it could do with double or triple the amount of washing machine.

If I am honest, would probably be the worst of the RAC's we have started at, and that's almost all of them, but still a very good park

Will stay again
Had a studio apartment. Just what we needed. Spotless. Cleaners get five stars. Booked three nights and had to ask for another night as we had car problems. Fourth night was half price. Wonderful.

Stayed here with the kids, our first camping trip. Booking process was efficient and easy to navigate. The sites was clean, well organised and the showers were great. Great facilities and great location close to amenemities.

We had a great stay with the grandchildren last weekend, the xmas light competition from other campers was really enjoyable and a pleasant surprise, everyone was so nice and made our stay even more enjoyable.
The camp site is great for families, pools and play equipment all well looked after.  We had a pitch with our own bathroom which was perfect for us with the children. We will definitely be back.
Enjoyed our time here, nice park, great for kids, 5 minute walk to the beach, also good beach for a swim, cheeky friendly  ducks, good camp kitchen, clean amenities
In a caravan and it was perfect for us
Stayed in our caravan, great park which is quiet and dog friendly. Very good facilities. Will definitely be using again.
Why can’t it be free
Spacious sites. Really clean. Quiet as.campsites go. Great toilet/shower facilities. Great location.
Great setting. A little astonished at the difference between Sat night and week night prices.
Very luxury camping. We spent 1 night there, we had a private bathroom. The kitchen is well equipment, the entertaining room was perfect for children.
Enjoyed the tent experience with our family. Kids love the swimming pool and giant pillow of bouncyness.
We had a wonderful stay! Very friendly staff, clean washrooms and overall a very nice campground.
Great place for a family holiday, plenty for the kids to do
Great for  a family holiday
뜨거운물 잘 나오고 전기 잘 나오고 평화롭고 넓고! 직원분 친절해요

Fabulous caravan park. Very easy access to our ensuite site and quite spacious compared to some parks so it doesn't feel like you're on top of one another. Easy to book and the staff were very helpful.
a wonderfully relaxing place. great facilities
Beautiful place and family friendly.

Had a few days in Busselton staying in a Queen studio.
Very clean, comfortable and roomy.
Great park facilities. Staff were very helpful and friendly. Will definitely stay again.
Very friendly staff at reception. Great amenities and the grounds are kept well maintained. Plenty of activities on site for kids to do.
This is the second time we have stayed here very welcoming caravan park, great value facilities are clean this time we were in powered caravan site which is well maintained.

Older camp big sites tidy most have grass with matting to keep the sand at Bay  where the grass is a bit 'thin' out of the main beach things to do areas in Busselton but sometimes that can be a good thing we stayed in an ensuite site which is not discounted like the normal powered sites - stay 3 pay for 2 very few if any permanents
I recommended to everyone come here for camping
Had everything we couldve wanted. Lovely receptionist, powered slab, en suite toilet and shower were clean with hot water and a fun games room. 0 complaints
Good caravan park. Shower block could use better shower heads, roads are gravel so not the best for kids scooters. Playground is great but not much for kids under 4.
Great place & nice and peaceful. Staff very helpful. One of the best parks in WA. Well done RAC.
The toilets have multi ply toilet paper. I dont need to say more.
Nicely presented areas where we stayed, we were late checking in, but the man came & was very polite and helpful. Being winter & wet, it wasn't busy at all, but still quite a few vans on site.
Great service from the staff and excellent facilities
Friendly staff and a great place for children.

Had a wonderful time. Very quiet as its off season, relaxing, very welcoming and knowledgeable staff.  Could possibly do with a few microwave containers and baking dishes. Accommodation is very affordable for families with lots of activities for kids
Fantastic
